subject: [DCAE] Fix PRH aai lookup url config
commit_message:
[DCAE] Fix PRH aai lookup url config

envsubst was modifying application config containing
${<pattern>} to null; introduced dummy variable as work-around
to generate required config

Change-Id: I596e542f4719880a4359de9ea82bbb80b9d87403
Signed-off-by: vv770d <vv770d@att.com>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2869
Signed-off-by: vv770d <vv770d@att.com>

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

that's hackish in my opinion. I'd prefer so that you pass explicitly --variables to env subst instead to make it more explicit and more obvious for the reader

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> that's hackish in my opinion. I'd prefer so that you pass explicitly --variables to env subst instead to make it more explicit and more obvious for the reader

Here the case is more complex.
We have a configuration property, where part of the path contains the $variables.

The envsubst gets a number of envs added to the container runtime and the idea is, that envsubst uses the env values to replace the $variables present in a configuration file (stored in a configMap).

PRH is probably the only component currently using the "$variables" in the configuration property, used as a path.

If we updated the envsubst container with envs corresponding to there parameters, the values for these envs would be really sth like: customer="$customer", tenant="$tenant".

Not sure, how that will really behave, but it could be an option overall too.
Your view?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> that's hackish in my opinion. I'd prefer so that you pass explicitly --variables to env subst instead to make it more explicit and more obvious for the reader

Hi Krzysztof - As Damian noted, the application (PRH) does the substitution based on incoming VES event data (hence actual values are not known at deployment time). As the pattern used for in-app substitution was same as envsubst container, the configurations were getting replaced before being made available to PRH app.

This is an one-off case; we are exploring other option to replace PRH in-app substitution via different pattern. 

Will be good if this can approved as this is blocking intergration smoke case (SO-3708 & SO-3710)

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > that's hackish in my opinion. I'd prefer so that you pass explicitly --variables to env subst instead to make it more explicit and more obvious for the reader
> 
> Hi Krzysztof - As Damian noted, the application (PRH) does the substitution based on incoming VES event data (hence actual values are not known at deployment time). As the pattern used for in-app substitution was same as envsubst container, the configurations were getting replaced before being made available to PRH app.
> 
> This is an one-off case; we are exploring other option to replace PRH in-app substitution via different pattern. 
> 
> Will be good if this can approved as this is blocking intergration smoke case (SO-3708 & SO-3710)

Ok. I can take it as a temporary but could you please add a comment to your config file to explain this so that stupid people like me would not need to scratch their heads too much when they read this in next year?;)

and BTW I'm not sure if you understood me correctly so let me explain it with an example from the net:
 $ cat /tmp/test
 VAR1=${VAR1}
 VAR2=${VAR2}
 VAR3=${VAR3}
 $ export  VAR1="one" VAR2="two" VAR3="three"
 $ envsubst '${VAR1} ${VAR3}' </tmp/test
 VAR1=one
 VAR2=${VAR2}
 VAR3=three

So with that you can simply avoid your special "$sth" string being replaced with nulls by envsubst

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > > 
> > > that's hackish in my opinion. I'd prefer so that you pass explicitly --variables to env subst instead to make it more explicit and more obvious for the reader
> > 
> > Hi Krzysztof - As Damian noted, the application (PRH) does the substitution based on incoming VES event data (hence actual values are not known at deployment time). As the pattern used for in-app substitution was same as envsubst container, the configurations were getting replaced before being made available to PRH app.
> > 
> > This is an one-off case; we are exploring other option to replace PRH in-app substitution via different pattern. 
> > 
> > Will be good if this can approved as this is blocking intergration smoke case (SO-3708 & SO-3710)
> 
> Ok. I can take it as a temporary but could you please add a comment to your config file to explain this so that stupid people like me would not need to scratch their heads too much when they read this in next year?;)
> 
> and BTW I'm not sure if you understood me correctly so let me explain it with an example from the net:
>  $ cat /tmp/test
>  VAR1=${VAR1}
>  VAR2=${VAR2}
>  VAR3=${VAR3}
>  $ export  VAR1="one" VAR2="two" VAR3="three"
>  $ envsubst '${VAR1} ${VAR3}' </tmp/test
>  VAR1=one
>  VAR2=${VAR2}
>  VAR3=three
> 
> So with that you can simply avoid your special "$sth" string being replaced with nulls by envsubst

Thanks Krzysztof.. Will add comment to explain this on config file. ;)

I did follow the solution you proposed however there is one constraint. The envsubst is invoked part of dcae common-service template (https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/dcaegen2-services/common/dcaegen2-services-common/templates/_deployment.tpl#n229) which is used by all DCAE components. We would have had to modify this generic template to exclude PRH specific parameters which may not have been clean approach either (if there were more one service needing such exclusion - common template could have been enhanced to take optional exclusion list - but this adds additional complexity and regression testing effort as well)
Uploaded patch set 3.

As the pattern used for in-app substitution was same as envsubst container, the configurations were getting replaced before being made available to PRH app.

> >  $ cat /tmp/test
> >  VAR1=${VAR1}
> >  VAR2=${VAR2}
> >  VAR3=${VAR3}
> >  $ export  VAR1="one" VAR2="two" VAR3="three"

> >  VAR1=one

> Thanks Krzysztof.. Will add comment to explain this on config file. ;)
> 
> I did follow the solution you proposed however there is one constraint. The envsubst is invoked part of dcae common-service template (https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/dcaegen2-services/common/dcaegen2-services-common/templates/_deployment.tpl#n229) which is used by all DCAE components. We would have had to modify this generic template to exclude PRH specific parameters which may not have been clean approach either (if there were more one service needing such exclusion - common template could have been enhanced to take optional exclusion list - but this adds additional complexity and regression testing effort as well)

Well personally I've been rather thinking about just adding to the common init container sth like:
 for var in `env | cut -d = -f 1`; do echo -n " \${$var}"; done

which would generate the string that can be passed to envsubst without the need of changing any other charts. This way we would replace only environment variables that are defined for this container, not all $random strings

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Looks good to me as a temporary solution (and I agree with Vijay).

The DCAE-SDK 1.8.6 (PRH uses currently 1.6.0) will resolve this issue permanently.
There is a plan to update the PRH code to change the default definition of variables in a path, and to migrate to that newest DCAE-SDK, but let`s fix the smoke tests first with this change.
Then we can really see after that DCAE-SDK update (1.6.0 -> 1.8.6), that we haven`t messed that thing up again.

If there is another PS here, then we can optionally fix the typo here.
